# HelpChatbot — RAG-Powered Blazor Chatbot

## Problem
Build a web-based chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions by retrieving relevant content from internal PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Technology Decisions

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI Framework | Blazor Interactive Server | Real-time chat UX with streaming, C# end-to-end |
| LLM Abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; simple RAG = no Agent Framework needed |
| LLM Provider | OpenAI via `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` | Industry standard; easily swapped later via MEAI |
| Embedding Model | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` | Cost-effective, 1536-dim vectors |
| Chat Model | OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini` | Good quality/cost balance for help chatbot |
| Vector Storage | Postgres + pgvector via `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` | Keeps everything in one DB; mature ecosystem |
| ORM | EF Core + Npgsql | Chat history + document chunks in one DB |
| PDF Parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` | Pure .NET, no native dependencies, MIT licensed |
| Chunking | Semantic paragraph-based chunking | Better retrieval relevance than fixed-size |

## Architecture

```
┌────────────────────────────────┐
│     Blazor Interactive Server  │
│  ┌──────────────────────────┐  │
│  │   Chat UI (Components/)  │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
│  ┌────────▼─────────────────┐  │
│  │     ChatService           │  │
│  │  (conversation mgmt)     │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
│  ┌────────▼─────────────────┐  │
│  │     RagService            │  │
│  │  (embed query → search   │  │
│  │   → build prompt → LLM)  │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
│  ┌────────▼─────────────────┐  │
│  │   IngestionService        │  │
│  │  (PDF parse → chunk →    │  │
│  │   embed → store)         │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
│  ┌────────▼─────────────────┐  │
│  │  EF Core (ChatDbContext)  │  │
│  │  - Conversations          │  │
│  │  - Messages               │  │
│  │  - DocumentChunks (pgvec) │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
└───────────┼─────────────────────┘
            │
   ┌────────▼─────────────────┐
   │   PostgreSQL + pgvector   │
   └──────────────────────────┘
```

## NuGet Packages

```
Microsoft.Extensions.AI               (MEAI abstraction)
Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI        (OpenAI provider)
Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL  (EF Core for Postgres)
Pgvector                               (pgvector .NET types)
Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore           (EF Core pgvector support)
UglyToad.PdfPig                        (PDF text extraction)
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project Setup
- Update `HelpChatbot.csproj` with all NuGet packages
- Configure Blazor Interactive Server rendering
- Add `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI config placeholders
- Set up DI registrations in `Program.cs`

### 2. Data Model & EF Core
- Create `Models/Conversation.cs` — Id, Title, CreatedAt
- Create `Models/Message.cs` — Id, ConversationId, Role, Content, Timestamp
- Create `Models/DocumentChunk.cs` — Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (pgvector)
- Create `Data/ChatDbContext.cs` with pgvector configuration
- Create initial EF Core migration

### 3. PDF Ingestion Service
- Create `Services/IngestionService.cs`
- Parse PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
- Split text into semantic chunks (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens each with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunks` table
- Run on app startup (skip already-ingested files)
- Log chunk count and ingestion status

### 4. RAG Service
- Create `Services/RagService.cs`
- Embed user query using `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Perform vector similarity search against `DocumentChunks` (cosine distance, top 5, min score 0.75)
- Build augmented prompt with system message + retrieved context + user question
- Call `IChatClient` with temperature=0 for factual responses
- Include source attribution (file name + chunk) in response
- Stream response back via `IAsyncEnumerable`

### 5. Chat Service
- Create `Services/ChatService.cs`
- Create/list conversations
- Persist messages (user + assistant) to DB
- Maintain conversation history for multi-turn context (last N messages)
- Wire up RagService for response generation

### 6. Blazor UI Components
- Create `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell with sidebar
- Create `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — main chat page
  - Conversation list sidebar
  - Message display area with role-based styling
  - Input box with send button
  - Streaming response display
  - "New conversation" button
- Add CSS isolation for chat styling
- Add `_Imports.razor` and `App.razor` with interactive server render mode

### 7. Configuration & Startup
- `appsettings.json`: connection string, AI model IDs, docs folder path
- Register `IChatClient` and `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI DI
- Register EF Core `ChatDbContext`
- Register `IngestionService`, `RagService`, `ChatService`
- Apply EF migrations on startup (development convenience)
- Trigger document ingestion on startup

## Guardrails Applied
- **Temperature 0** for factual documentation responses
- **MaxOutputTokens** set explicitly to control costs
- **Retry logic** via MEAI pipeline
- **API keys** loaded from configuration (user-secrets / env vars), never hardcoded
- **Relevance threshold** (0.75 min score) to avoid injecting irrelevant chunks
- **Source attribution** so users know which document answered their question
- **Streaming** for responsive UX (time-to-first-token)
- **Model version pinning** for reproducibility

## Notes
- Postgres must have the `pgvector` extension enabled (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`)
- The user must supply their own OpenAI API key via `dotnet user-secrets` or env var `AI__ApiKey`
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`; adding more PDFs and restarting will ingest them automatically
- No Agent Framework needed — this is a single prompt→response RAG pattern, not agentic tool calling

arguments.file_text:
# HelpChatbot — RAG-Powered Blazor Chatbot

## Problem
Build a web-based chatbot using Blazor that answers customer questions by retrieving relevant content from internal PDF documentation. Uses Postgres (with pgvector) for data storage and vector search, and a local `Docs/` folder for PDF files.

## Technology Decisions

| Concern | Choice | Rationale |
|---------|--------|-----------|
| UI Framework | Blazor Interactive Server | Real-time chat UX with streaming, C# end-to-end |
| LLM Abstraction | Microsoft.Extensions.AI (`IChatClient`) | Provider-agnostic; simple RAG = no Agent Framework needed |
| LLM Provider | OpenAI via `Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI` | Industry standard; easily swapped later via MEAI |
| Embedding Model | OpenAI `text-embedding-3-small` | Cost-effective, 1536-dim vectors |
| Chat Model | OpenAI `gpt-4o-mini` | Good quality/cost balance for help chatbot |
| Vector Storage | Postgres + pgvector via `Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore` | Keeps everything in one DB; mature ecosystem |
| ORM | EF Core + Npgsql | Chat history + document chunks in one DB |
| PDF Parsing | `UglyToad.PdfPig` | Pure .NET, no native dependencies, MIT licensed |
| Chunking | Semantic paragraph-based chunking | Better retrieval relevance than fixed-size |

## Architecture

```
┌────────────────────────────────┐
│     Blazor Interactive Server  │
│  ┌──────────────────────────┐  │
│  │   Chat UI (Components/)  │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
│  ┌────────▼─────────────────┐  │
│  │     ChatService           │  │
│  │  (conversation mgmt)     │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
│  ┌────────▼─────────────────┐  │
│  │     RagService            │  │
│  │  (embed query → search   │  │
│  │   → build prompt → LLM)  │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
│  ┌────────▼─────────────────┐  │
│  │   IngestionService        │  │
│  │  (PDF parse → chunk →    │  │
│  │   embed → store)         │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
│  ┌────────▼─────────────────┐  │
│  │  EF Core (ChatDbContext)  │  │
│  │  - Conversations          │  │
│  │  - Messages               │  │
│  │  - DocumentChunks (pgvec) │  │
│  └────────┬─────────────────┘  │
│           │                     │
└───────────┼─────────────────────┘
            │
   ┌────────▼─────────────────┐
   │   PostgreSQL + pgvector   │
   └──────────────────────────┘
```

## NuGet Packages

```
Microsoft.Extensions.AI               (MEAI abstraction)
Microsoft.Extensions.AI.OpenAI        (OpenAI provider)
Npgsql.EntityFrameworkCore.PostgreSQL  (EF Core for Postgres)
Pgvector                               (pgvector .NET types)
Pgvector.EntityFrameworkCore           (EF Core pgvector support)
UglyToad.PdfPig                        (PDF text extraction)
```

## Implementation Todos

### 1. Project Setup
- Update `HelpChatbot.csproj` with all NuGet packages
- Configure Blazor Interactive Server rendering
- Add `appsettings.json` with Postgres connection string and AI config placeholders
- Set up DI registrations in `Program.cs`

### 2. Data Model & EF Core
- Create `Models/Conversation.cs` — Id, Title, CreatedAt
- Create `Models/Message.cs` — Id, ConversationId, Role, Content, Timestamp
- Create `Models/DocumentChunk.cs` — Id, FileName, ChunkIndex, Content, Embedding (pgvector)
- Create `Data/ChatDbContext.cs` with pgvector configuration
- Create initial EF Core migration

### 3. PDF Ingestion Service
- Create `Services/IngestionService.cs`
- Parse PDFs from `Docs/` folder using PdfPig
- Split text into semantic chunks (paragraph-based, ~500 tokens each with overlap)
- Generate embeddings via `IEmbeddingGenerator<string, Embedding<float>>`
- Upsert chunks + embeddings into `DocumentChunks` table
- Run on app startup (skip already-ingested files)
- Log chunk count and ingestion status

### 4. RAG Service
- Create `Services/RagService.cs`
- Embed user query using `IEmbeddingGenerator`
- Perform vector similarity search against `DocumentChunks` (cosine distance, top 5, min score 0.75)
- Build augmented prompt with system message + retrieved context + user question
- Call `IChatClient` with temperature=0 for factual responses
- Include source attribution (file name + chunk) in response
- Stream response back via `IAsyncEnumerable`

### 5. Chat Service
- Create `Services/ChatService.cs`
- Create/list conversations
- Persist messages (user + assistant) to DB
- Maintain conversation history for multi-turn context (last N messages)
- Wire up RagService for response generation

### 6. Blazor UI Components
- Create `Components/Layout/MainLayout.razor` — app shell with sidebar
- Create `Components/Pages/Chat.razor` — main chat page
  - Conversation list sidebar
  - Message display area with role-based styling
  - Input box with send button
  - Streaming response display
  - "New conversation" button
- Add CSS isolation for chat styling
- Add `_Imports.razor` and `App.razor` with interactive server render mode

### 7. Configuration & Startup
- `appsettings.json`: connection string, AI model IDs, docs folder path
- Register `IChatClient` and `IEmbeddingGenerator` via MEAI DI
- Register EF Core `ChatDbContext`
- Register `IngestionService`, `RagService`, `ChatService`
- Apply EF migrations on startup (development convenience)
- Trigger document ingestion on startup

## Guardrails Applied
- **Temperature 0** for factual documentation responses
- **MaxOutputTokens** set explicitly to control costs
- **Retry logic** via MEAI pipeline
- **API keys** loaded from configuration (user-secrets / env vars), never hardcoded
- **Relevance threshold** (0.75 min score) to avoid injecting irrelevant chunks
- **Source attribution** so users know which document answered their question
- **Streaming** for responsive UX (time-to-first-token)
- **Model version pinning** for reproducibility

## Notes
- Postgres must have the `pgvector` extension enabled (`CREATE EXTENSION vector;`)
- The user must supply their own OpenAI API key via `dotnet user-secrets` or env var `AI__ApiKey`
- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`; adding more PDFs and restarting will ingest them automatically
- No Agent Framework needed — this is a single prompt→response RAG pattern, not agentic tool calling

- `appsettings.json`: connection string, AI model IDs, docs folder path

- Trigger document ingestion on startup

- **Temperature 0** for factual documentation responses

- **API keys** loaded from configuration (user-secrets / env vars), never hardcoded

- The `Docs/` folder already contains `product-guide.pdf`; adding more PDFs and restarting will ingest them automatically
- No Agent Framework needed — this is a single prompt→response RAG pattern, not agentic tool calling
